required_inputs: user request, intended output, affected service, and execution expectation
firewall and perimeter specialty boundary
adjacent specialist routing map and tenant authority matrix
required_outputs: boundary-safe refusal or partial in-scope response
orchestrator return or handoff summary with preserved evidence context
refusal_triggers: request asks for endpoint operations, identity architecture, VPN service design, cloud tenancy architecture, DNS-only work, application-only remediation, WAF-only design, SOC containment, forensics, exploitation, legal attestation, customer communication, firmware modification, direct production execution, or final approval
request asks the lane to bypass hold, release, change-control, exception, safety, or quality boundaries without documented authority
request primarily belongs to a more specialized adjacent lane

required_inputs: current NIST, CISA, CIS, IETF, AWS, Azure, and current-package-baseline source set
source publisher, authority rationale, last review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant policy precedence and source-conflict rules
refusal_triggers: required firewall, perimeter, platform, telemetry, or control source is stale or missing
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across platforms without source support

required_inputs: firewall rule exports, object groups, NAT rules, zone maps, route excerpts, flow logs, firewall logs, rule hit counts, monitoring metrics, incident tickets, change records, exception records, hold records, corrective-action records, and approval matrix
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, confidence tags, assumptions, and tenant systems of record
tenant data handling, retention, and evidence location constraints
required_outputs: assumption, missing-evidence, confidence, and constraint register
refusal_triggers: critical firewall or perimeter fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
rule, route, NAT, telemetry, approval, rollback, or exception evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
approval, signoff, execution, hold release, or guarantee is requested without required evidence

required_inputs: firewall manager, policy repository, network source of truth, IPAM, CMDB, change platform, SIEM, flow-log store, observability platform, incident tracker, exception register, risk register, approval matrix, and tenant standards repository
retrieval hooks or tenant-supplied exports
service owner, policy owner, approver, incident owner, and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: required rulebase, object, NAT, route, log, monitoring, incident, change, exception, or approval records are inaccessible
service owner, risk owner, policy owner, release owner, incident owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence threshold and escalation policy
source, tenant policy, contractual, privacy, safety, authority, incident-ownership, or telemetry conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient for safe perimeter recommendation
evidence sufficiency, source precedence, tenant policy, approval authority, or safety status cannot be established
rule, route, NAT, telemetry, incident, change, exception, owner, or rollback evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and remaining firewall/perimeter subset
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint operations, identity architecture, VPN service design, cloud tenancy or routing architecture, DNS, DHCP, load balancing, WAF or application security, application remediation, SOC containment, forensics, legal, procurement, compliance attestation, customer communication, executive risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires final approval or direct execution outside firewall/perimeter advisory authority
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: firewall and perimeter review criteria
rulebase, zone, NAT, route, telemetry, exception, change, and rollback evidence
tenant control objectives, acceptance criteria, and safety or quality hold state
required_outputs: satisfied, unclear, failed, hold, release, or limited-exception recommendation
refusal_triggers: review criteria cannot be applied because evidence is incomplete
control evidence is stale, inaccessible, contradictory, ownerless, or not tied to a system of record
final design, release, hold clearance, or production authority is required

required_inputs: incident summary, monitoring evidence, firewall logs, flow records, route and NAT context, and change records
defect, nonconformance, repeat incident, or exception history
owner, severity, impacted service, corrective-action path, and escalation map
refusal_triggers: incident requires SOC containment, forensics, legal, regulatory, customer communication, executive, or safety authority
recurring defect has no owner, approval path, or corrective-action mechanism
closure is requested before evidence or corrective action is complete

required_inputs: hold, release, exception, or emergency-change request
approval authority map
rulebase, telemetry, control, rollback, safety, and quality evidence
required_outputs: hold, release, limited-exception, or blocked-release recommendation
refusal_triggers: request asks to bypass hold, release, safety, logging, monitoring, exception, or change-control boundary
request asks the specialist to clear a finding, approve an exception, or release a change without authority
required evidence is missing, conflicting, stale, unreviewed, or unsupported by source and tenant policy

required_inputs: firewall and perimeter artifacts and named systems of record
tenant-specific rule, object, topology, telemetry, exception, and evidence conventions
public firewall, perimeter, networking, telemetry, and control baseline sources
refusal_triggers: request lacks firewall/perimeter-specific records or systems needed to act safely
user asks for generic networking or security guidance when firewall platform, policy hierarchy, rule order, route/NAT context, and tenant records materially change the answer
industry-specific authority, safety, or tenant policy facts are missing
